stories.headline: Iran halts US talks and threatens Hormuz and Bab al-Mandab over Israel's Lebanon offensive; CENTCOM intercepts missiles aimed at Kuwait
stories.summary: On June 1, Iran suspended its US-mediated messaging and FM Araghchi declared the Iran-US ceasefire applies 'on all fronts, including in Lebanon,' holding Washington and Israel responsible for consequences. IRGC figures threatened to disrupt the Strait of Hormuz and Bab al-Mandab, and CENTCOM said it intercepted Iranian missiles aimed at US bases in Kuwait. Trump countered that talks continue 'at a rapid pace' and that he 'couldn't care less' if they end; oil spiked more than 6%.

perspectives.keyFraming: Iran ties the fragile US truce to Lebanon, suspending back-channel talks as escalation risks widening into a regional war.
perspectives.summary: France 24's live coverage reports Iran stopped exchanging mediated messages with the US, citing intensified IDF attacks on Hezbollah, while the US and Israel insist the Iran truce excludes Lebanon operations. The move injects fresh uncertainty into a deal Trump has not signed, with Tehran framing the Lebanon front as a tripwire for the broader ceasefire.

sources.title: Middle East live: Iran suspends mediated negotiations over Lebanon attacks

perspectives.keyFraming: Tehran threatens 'consequences,' reframing a bilateral truce as a region-wide tripwire that any Lebanon strike could trigger.
perspectives.summary: The Times of Israel's liveblog records Araghchi's threat that any violation in Lebanon breaches the US-Iran truce and Iran's halt to mediated messaging. Israeli coverage frames the statement as Tehran leveraging the ceasefire to constrain Israeli operations against Hezbollah while preserving its own deterrence posture.

sources.title: Tehran says Israeli strikes in Lebanon violate US-Iran truce, threatens 'consequences'

perspectives.keyFraming: Iran's foreign minister directly threatens the US and Israel; pro-Israel outlets cast it as Tehran weaponizing the truce.
perspectives.summary: JNS reports Araghchi holding the US and Israel 'responsible for any violation of truce, including in Lebanon,' underscoring Tehran's 'all-fronts' doctrine. The framing presents Iran as trying to extend its Lebanon proxy's protection through diplomatic leverage even as US forces conducted weekend self-defense strikes on Iranian sites.

sources.title: Iranian FM: US, Israel responsible for 'any violation' of truce, including in Lebanon

stories.headline: Netanyahu orders strikes on Beirut's southern suburbs as Israeli troops reach deepest point in Lebanon in 26 years
stories.summary: On June 1, Netanyahu and Defense Minister Katz ordered IDF strikes on Hezbollah targets in Dahiyeh, south Beirut, after a weekend advance past the Litani and the capture of Beaufort Castle. Thousands fled as Hezbollah fired rockets toward northern Israel. France sought an emergency UN Security Council meeting; Washington floated a de-escalation roadmap ahead of Lebanon-Israel talks June 2-3. Lebanon reports 3,400+ killed since March.

perspectives.keyFraming: Israel expands its assault into Beirut after the deepest ground push since 2000; civilians flee Dahiyeh as Washington scrambles a ceasefire roadmap.
perspectives.summary: Al Jazeera and UPI report Israel ordered strikes on Dahiyeh after advancing past the Litani and seizing Beaufort Castle, the farthest north Israeli forces have operated in Lebanon in 26 years. Hezbollah fired rockets toward Haifa's outskirts as residents fled the southern suburbs. A US official said Washington was circulating a de-escalation roadmap to both sides.

sources.title: Lebanese start fleeing south Beirut after Israel says it will target the area
Netanyahu orders Beirut strikes after Lebanon advance

perspectives.keyFraming: A brittle truce shatters; Israel signals 'no calm in Beirut' while vowing a military-controlled zone in southern Lebanon.
perspectives.summary: The Washington Post reports Israel's defense minister vowed a controlled zone in southern Lebanon and warned of 'no calm in Beirut' if Hezbollah attacks continue, as rockets struck northern Israel. Coverage notes fears among Lebanese that the 'Gaza model' of sustained operations is being replicated, with displacement spreading from the south to the capital's outskirts.

sources.title: Israel orders strikes on Beirut's southern suburbs as Hezbollah rockets hit northern Israel

perspectives.keyFraming: Netanyahu casts the strikes as a justified response to repeated Hezbollah ceasefire violations and rocket attacks on Israeli cities.
perspectives.summary: The Washington Times reports Netanyahu said the strikes followed 'repeated violations of the cease-fire in Lebanon by the Hezbollah terrorist organization and the attacks against our cities and citizens.' The order, issued jointly with Defense Minister Katz, came after the IDF raised the Israeli flag over Beaufort Castle and pushed north of the Litani River.

sources.title: Israeli PM Netanyahu orders strikes on Hezbollah in Beirut; IDF captures Beaufort Castle

perspectives.keyFraming: Chinese state media stresses Israel's plan for a military-controlled zone to the Litani and foregrounds the Lebanese casualty toll.
perspectives.summary: Xinhua reports Israel intends to establish a military-controlled zone around Lebanon's Litani River and that Katz warned of 'no peace in Beirut' until Hezbollah halts attacks. State coverage foregrounds the Lebanese Public Health Emergency Operations Center's figure of more than 3,400 killed and 10,200 wounded since early March, framing the offensive as open-ended occupation.

sources.title: Israel vows to create military-controlled zone around Lebanon's Litani River

stories.headline: Oil jumps and US stocks slip after Iran halts US talks and threatens to fully close the Strait of Hormuz
stories.summary: Crude surged on June 1 after Iranian state media said Tehran halted mediated messaging with Washington and threatened to fully block the Strait of Hormuz: Brent jumped more than 6% toward $97 and WTI over 7% above $94 intraday. Prices pared gains after Trump told CNBC he 'doesn't care' if talks end but later said Israel and Hezbollah agreed to halt attacks; Brent settled near $96 and WTI near $90-92. Equities shrugged off the shock to close at records on tech strength.

perspectives.keyFraming: Supply-shock mechanics: a renewed Hormuz threat puts roughly a fifth of seaborne crude at risk, reviving inflation and rate worries.
perspectives.summary: CNBC and Yahoo Finance report US crude jumped more than 6% intraday on the report that Iran would halt talks and move to close Hormuz, with Brent up around 5%. Coverage frames geopolitics as overriding May's record equity rally; traders re-priced Fed expectations as the conflict's energy-price channel reasserted itself, with safe-haven gold and the dollar firming.

sources.title: U.S. oil jumps more than 6% on report Iran will halt talks with U.S. and completely block Hormuz
Stock market today: Dow, S&P 500, Nasdaq waver as oil surges

perspectives.keyFraming: Markets stay resilient — tech strength from Computex cushions the energy hit, and the administration signals the Iran deal may still hold.
perspectives.summary: TheStreet's market blog notes that after May's record-setting run, US futures wavered rather than tumbled, with Computex-driven tech optimism offsetting energy and small-cap weakness. The lean-right read emphasizes Trump's confidence that the situation 'will all work out' and treats the oil spike as a geopolitical premium rather than a fundamental shift in the disinflation trajectory.

sources.title: Stock Market Today (June 1, 2026): Nasdaq Futures Rise After May's Record-Setting Run

stories.headline: US stocks open June at fresh records: Dow tops 51,000, Nasdaq clears 27,000 for the first time as Nvidia leads tech
stories.summary: All three major US indexes closed at records to open June on Monday: the S&P 500 rose 0.26% to 7,599.96, the Nasdaq added 0.42% to 27,086.81 — its first close above 27,000 — and the Dow gained 0.09% to 51,078.88. A Nvidia-led tech rally, powered by its Computex chip launch, overpowered a sharp intraday oil spike on Iran/Hormuz fears, with late-session US-Iran optimism helping indexes hold gains.

perspectives.keyFraming: Tech strength and AI optimism outweighed an energy shock, extending the record run despite Middle East risk.
perspectives.summary: Indexes notched fresh highs as Nvidia's RTX Spark chip launch powered tech and investors bet a US-Iran deal could still hold. The Nasdaq cleared 27,000 for the first time and the Dow held above 51,000, weeks after first crossing 50,000. The advance came even as crude jumped sharply intraday, underscoring the dominance of AI-linked megacaps in driving index returns.

sources.title: Stock market today: Dow, S&P 500, Nasdaq clinch records as Nvidia surges, US-Iran optimism returns
S&P 500 closes at a record to kick off June trading as tech rally overpowers oil spike: Live updates

stories.headline: California's top-two gubernatorial primary on June 2 features a crowded field and a possible Democratic lockout
stories.summary: California votes June 2 in a top-two primary to succeed term-limited Gov. Gavin Newsom from a field of about 61 candidates; the two highest finishers advance regardless of party. Final polls show Democrat Xavier Becerra leading, with Democrat Tom Steyer and Republican Steve Hilton battling for the second slot and Katie Porter and Chad Bianco trailing. Candidates made closing pitches over the weekend after Rep. Eric Swalwell's exit reshaped the Democratic field.

perspectives.keyFraming: A crowded jungle primary where the real suspense is who joins frontrunner Becerra in the November runoff.
perspectives.summary: PBS NewsHour reports California governor and LA mayor candidates making their final pitches before June 2, with only the two highest vote-getters advancing regardless of party. Becerra leans on decades in office, Steyer touts his self-funded independence, and Hilton pitches deregulation and lower costs in a fragmented field where the order of finish is decisive.

sources.title: Candidates for California governor and LA mayor make their final pitches to voters

perspectives.keyFraming: Emphasizes Becerra's surge and the real danger of Democrats getting locked out of the runoff if their vote fragments.
perspectives.summary: KQED reports Becerra and Hilton leading recent polling and frames the top-two dynamics as high-stakes for Democrats, who could in theory be shut out of November if too many strong Democratic candidates split the vote. The piece situates the contest as the marquee race to define post-Newsom California.

sources.title: Becerra, Hilton Lead in California Governor's Race Poll Ahead of June Primary

perspectives.keyFraming: Republicans Hilton and Bianco vie to be the GOP standard-bearer in a blue state, pitching affordability and deregulation.
perspectives.summary: KTLA-area coverage frames the race narrowing to three frontrunners, with Trump-endorsed Steve Hilton positioning as an outsider promising to cut regulation and bring down housing and energy costs, and Riverside County Sheriff Chad Bianco appealing to the conservative base. The GOP aim is to claim one of the two runoff slots out of the crowded field.

sources.title: New poll shows California governor race down to 3 frontrunners
